perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and (ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information. Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confide ntial BCSI .”
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) only written information explicitly designated as such by VELCO and labeled “Confidential BCSI”, furnished after the date hereof; (ii) No other information shall be considered BCSI.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines BCSI as all information designated by VELCO, regardless of form (oral, written, etc.) or timing (before or after the agreement). The modified version restricts BCSI to only written information labeled as "Confidential BCSI" and furnished after the agreement date. This contradicts the original broad definition and creates uncertainty. The definition of BCSI being only written and labeled provided after the date of the agreement directly contradicts with Section IV Use and Protection of Classified Information, number 2, where is talking about ANY NOTES made by Recipient containing Classified Information. If ANY notes contain BCSI, BCSI can't be only written.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'BCSI' to only written information explicitly designated as such and labeled 'Confidential BCSI', furnished after the agreement date. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to 'Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information.' If BCSI is ONLY written information, then ANY notes made by recipient can't contain BCSI, and that creates a direct, strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, CEII shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and ( ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information. Written information containing CEII that is furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confidential CEII ”.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, CEII shall mean: (i) only information explicitly identified by VELCO in writing as CEII *and* subsequently published in a peer-reviewed journal; (ii) No other information shall be considered CEII.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of CEII includes all information designated by VELCO as such, regardless of its form or whether it has been publicly vetted. The modified definition restricts CEII to information that is not only designated by VELCO in writing but also published in a peer-reviewed journal. This narrower definition is inconsistent with Section IV, paragraph 5, regarding disclosing CEII by subpoena. If CEII must be peer-reviewed before it can be considered CEII, it will create an in-text contradiction with section IV where VELCO has to take action in the event of the disclosure by subpoena.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of CEII to information explicitly identified in writing *and* published in a peer-reviewed journal. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines procedures for disclosing 'Classified Information' (defined by the original CEII definition) under subpoena. The changed definition makes it impossible to comply with the subpoena clause because information not yet peer-reviewed cannot be CEII under the new definition, creating a direct conflict and potential unenforceability, given that the original text allows for a much broader scope of information to be considered CEII. This aligns with the definition of structural flaws causing in-text contradictions and inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential . CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean *only* information explicitly marked and identified as “Confidential” by VELCO *at the time* of disclosure to the Recipient and included in a list of CI provided to recipient . It *excludes* information of third parties in VELCO’s possession and any information not included in the list. CI subject to this Agreement will only be the one from the list and in written form.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition encompasses a broad range of confidential information, including that of third parties and both tangible and intangible forms. The modified version limits CI to information explicitly marked as "Confidential" at disclosure, included in a list provided to the recipient, and excludes third-party information. This contradicts Section IV, paragraph 3, which refers to the Recipient exercising reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of all Classified Information. By limiting CI to the one explicitly marked at the time of disclosure, VELCO is not responsible for any other classified information, and that contradicts with paragraph 3 in Section IV.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'CI' compared to the original. It limits it to explicitly marked information, included in a list, and excludes third-party information, and limits the form to written only. The contradicted text states that the Recipient must maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of 'Classified Information'. The modification makes VELCO responsible for only a small subset of classified information, directly contradicting the Recipient's responsibility outlined in the Contradicted Text to protect *all* Classified Information. This creates a direct and significant conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place; however, the recipient is not responsible for securing oral classified information. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipients who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material *only* if the Classified Information is labeled as 'Confidential'. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information *are not included* in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Recipient shall keep *all* Classified Information in a secure place and that notes containing Classified Information *shall* be marked and designated accordingly. The modified text adds that the recipient is not responsible for securing oral classified information, and says that Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information *are not included* in this Agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty as to what kind of classified information should be marked as confidential, and if notes are not considered classified, they can be divulgated. The contradiction impacts Section IV paragraph 6, because how would the Recipient follow VELCO's instructions if the notes aren't part of the classified information?
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof, whether electronic or paper media.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that *all* Classified Information must be kept secure and that notes containing classified information *shall* be marked. The changed text now excludes oral classified information from the security requirement and states that notes are *not included* in the agreement. This contradicts the Contradicted Text (Section IV paragraph 6) which states that the Recipient will return or destroy *all* Classified Information, including *all* copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof. By excluding notes and oral information, the changed text creates a situation where the Recipient may not be required to return or destroy all classified information as VELCO requests, leading to a direct conflict and impacting the enforeceability of the original terms.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information, but only to the extent that the Recipient deems reasonable. The Recipient may divulge Classified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of VELCO *if* it is in the public interest, as determined solely by the Recipient. The Recipient is *not* responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Recipient to exercise reasonable care in maintaining confidentiality and prohibits divulging Classified Information without VELCO's consent. It also holds the Recipient responsible for its Representatives' breaches. The modified version grants the Recipient discretion in determining what is 'reasonable care,' permits divulging information in the 'public interest' (as self-defined), and removes responsibility for Representatives' breaches. By including that is in the 'public interest', the recipient can divulgated when he wants it, so Section IV number 5 becomes unnecessary because the Recipient can divulgated by deciding it is in the 'public interest'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions with the contradicted text. First, the changed text allows the Recipient to divulge Classified Information if it is in the 'public interest', as determined solely by the Recipient. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies a procedure for disclosing Classified Information required by law or subpoena, involving notice to VELCO, cooperation in seeking a protective order, and consultation with VELCO. The changed text bypasses this entire procedure, giving the Recipient unilateral authority to disclose information based on their own definition of 'public interest', making Section IV number 5 (Contradicted Text) unnecessary. Also, by giving the Recipient discretion in divulging information, the Recipient may not need to consult with VELCO. This creates a direct conflict in obligations, meeting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If there is a change in status of the Recipient to his/her employer, he/she must inform VELCO immediately in writing at the addr ess given above (Attention: Information Protection Coordinator ) and follow VELCO’s instructions with respect to disposition of the Classified Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If there is a change in status of the Recipient to his/her employer, he/she *may* inform VELCO immediately in writing at the address given above (Attention: Information Protection Coordinator ) and *may or may not* follow VELCO’s instructions with respect to disposition of the Classified Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the recipient *must* inform VELCO immediately of a change in employment status and *must* follow VELCO's instructions for disposing of Classified Information. The modified version makes both of these actions optional ('may inform,' 'may or may not follow'). This directly contradicts the survival clause in section IV, number 11, because the classification is not rescinded, even if the recipient has a status change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient/ remains bound by these provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the Recipient 'must inform' and 'must follow' VELCO's instructions. The changed text weakens these obligations to 'may inform' and 'may or may not follow.' This creates a contradiction because the 'Recipient remains bound' unless VELCO rescinds the designation, as stated in the Contradicted Text. By making the recipient's actions optional, the changed text allows the recipient to disregard the requirement to inform VELCO and follow its instructions, which conflicts with the condition that they remain bound by the provisions unless VELCO rescinds the designation. Thus, there is a clear contradiction.
